title: Extracting total RNA from Caenorhabditis elegans using phase-lock gel separation tubes
author: Cristian Riccio
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.5sug6ew
materials: Continue with Nanodrop and RNA Broad Range Qubit. Info on phase lock tubes: Quantabio, 5PRIME, Phase Lock Gel Heavy, 2ml – 200, catalog number: 2302830, store at 15-25 degrees.Ordered more from VWR International Ltd., ref. 733-2478
Info on phase lock tubes: Quantabio, 5PRIME, Phase Lock Gel Heavy, 2ml – 200, catalog number: 2302830, store at 15-25 degrees. Ordered more from VWR International Ltd., ref. 733-2478

steps.title: Start with a 90mm NGM plate containing 3300 worms
steps.instruction: Start with a 90mm NGM plate containing 3300 worms.

steps.title: Add M9 to the plate and...
steps.instruction: Add M9 to the plate and collect the worms into a 14 ml Falcon tube

steps.title: Centrifuge at 2000 rpm in big centrifuge
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 2000 rpm in big centrifuge.

steps.title: Remove supernatant
steps.instruction: Remove supernatant.

steps.title: Add M9 to Falcon to resuspend worm pellet
steps.instruction: Add M9 to Falcon to resuspend worm pellet.

steps.title: Repeat steps 5-7 three times
steps.instruction: Repeat steps 5-7 three times.

steps.title: Remove supernatant
steps.instruction: Remove supernatant.

steps.title: Add 750 ul TRIsure (Bioline, catalog...
steps.instruction: Add 750 ul TRIsure (Bioline, catalog number BIO-38033) to samples and store in 80 C freezer.

steps.title: Decontaminate the working area with RNAseZap or 0
steps.instruction: Decontaminate the working area with RNAseZap or 0.1% SDS in DEPC water.

steps.title: 5 times freeze-thaw cycles in the TRIsure in liquid nitrogen
steps.instruction: 5 times freeze-thaw cycles in the TRIsure in liquid nitrogen. Dip the tube in liquid nitrogen for 3 minutes. Dip the tube in water at room temperature until no ice can be seen anymore. For 24 samples, 3 tanks of liquid nitrogen are useful.

steps.title: Make sure there are very few...
steps.instruction: Make sure there are very few carcasses left in the Trisure.

steps.title: If after 5 freeze-thaw cycles, there...
steps.instruction: If after 5 freeze-thaw cycles, there are still many carcasses in the TriSURE, consider adding 750 ul TriSURE and splitting each sample into 2.

steps.title: pre-spin phase-lock tubes at 21,130 for...
steps.instruction: pre-spin phase-lock tubes at 21,130 for 1 min at 4 C.

steps.title: Transfer Trisure/worms to a Heavy phase lock gel tube (pipet),
steps.instruction: Transfer Trisure/worms to a Heavy phase lock gel tube (pipet),

steps.title: Add 150 ul chloroform, shake by...
steps.instruction: Add 150 ul chloroform, shake by hand (until emulsion) and incubate for 10 min at RT. After 10 minutes, there are 3 phases. The gel is at the bottom, the green phase is in the middle and a pale/clear phase is at the top. Do not store chloroform in plastic! Chloroform penetrates plastic very efficiently and gets out of falcon tubes. Use chloroform straight out of the glass jar.

steps.title: Centrifuge at 21,130 x g for...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 21,130 x g for 5 minutes at 4 C.

steps.title: Add 400 ul chloroform using the...
steps.instruction: Add 400 ul chloroform using the manual repeater pipette with a 10 ml combitip. I experienced no spillage from the 10 ml combitip as opposed to normal tips/P1000. However, the combitip pipette can be splashy if the sliding mechanism is not smooth.

steps.title: Centrifuge at 21,130 x g for...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 21,130 x g for 5 minutes at 4 C.

steps.title: Now, the positions of the gel...
steps.instruction: Now, the positions of the gel and the green phase have exchanged. The gel is in the middle, with the pale phase at the top and the green phase at the bottom.

steps.title: Collect upper phase (decant) into a...
steps.instruction: Collect upper phase (decant) into a DNA LoBind tube (leave some in order to avoid taking gel). Add 500 ul isopropanol (meaning 1:1 aqueous phase-isopropanol) and 1 ul glycogen (optional for abundant RNA = no need to add if RNA is abundant). Mix the tube by inversion. Incubate for 5 minutes at room temperature (RT for abundant RNA, -80 C for one hour for low qty RNA). A postdoc in the lab suggests to not incubate it on ice or in the freezer because that might precipitate salts. The isopropanol/aqueous phase solution can appear granular. The granules are the precipitated RNA.

steps.title: Centrifuge at maximum speed for 30...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at maximum speed for 30 min at 4 C (=21,130 x g in Eppendorf Centrifuge 5424 R).

steps.title: Heat DEPC water at 80 C in heat block
steps.instruction: Heat DEPC water at 80 C in heat block.

steps.title: Throw away the phase-lock tubes into their own waste bin
steps.instruction: Throw away the phase-lock tubes into their own waste bin.

steps.title: Remove isopropanol
steps.instruction: Remove isopropanol.

steps.title: Wash 4 times with 900 ul...
steps.instruction: Wash 4 times with 900 ul of cold (keep on dry ice) freshly prepared 70% EtOH (make sure to remove as much supernatant as possible but do not let the pellets dry). Prepare the 70% EtOH fresh each time because ethanol solutions are hygroscopic and can thus decrease in ethanol content over time. The washing works as follows: add 900 ul ethanol, centrifuge 5 minutes at 4 C 21,000 x g and remove ethanol.Note: for small RNA extraction, 80% EtOH is better. However, the Sanger small RNA seq facility receives total RNA and then does a size selection to isolate small RNA, so use 70% EtOH.

steps.title: Centrifuge 5 minutes at 4 C...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge 5 minutes at 4 C top speed (21,130 x g) and remove supernatant.

steps.title: First remove supernatant with P1000 on 1000 ul
steps.instruction: First remove supernatant with P1000 on 1000 ul. Then, remove it with the P20 at 20 ul. If the pellet detaches, centrifuge again for 2 minutes. Keep the eppendorfs with the opening in the inside to locate the pellet more easily.

steps.title: Air-dry in the hood for 10 minutes at room temperature
steps.instruction: Air-dry in the hood for 10 minutes at room temperature. A tiny drop of ethanol left after drying does not correlate with low RNA or contamination.

steps.title: Resuspend in 50 ul 80 C...
steps.instruction: Resuspend in 50 ul 80 C DEPC water (because the Sanger Institute facility accepts a volume of up to 50 ul).

steps.title: Flick tube for 1 minute
steps.instruction: Flick tube for 1 minute. If many samples are being processed at once, take 3-4 in a hand and scrape the bottom of the tubes on a rack back and forth.

steps.title: Heat tube in heat block for...
steps.instruction: Heat tube in heat block for 1 minute at 80 C.

steps.title: Spin down
steps.instruction: Spin down.

steps.title: Transfer tubes on water ice
steps.instruction: Transfer tubes on water ice. A PhD student in the lab advises to transfer on ice from aqueous phase onwards but the time I did not use ice, the RNA extraction worked and the RNA was good quality (Bioanalyser Nano).

steps.title: Note
steps.instruction: Note: for small RNA extraction, 80% EtOH is better. However, the Sanger small RNA seq facility receives total RNA and then does a size selection to isolate small RNA, so use 70% EtOH.

steps.title: phaseLockTubes
steps.instruction: phaseLockTubes.docx PLG.pdf trisure_fact_sheet.pdf trisure_product_manual.pdf trisure.jpg MAN0017209_Qubit_4_Fluorometer_UG.pdf MAN0017210_Qubit_4_Assays_QR.pdf QRC10210.pdf Qubit_RNA_BR_Assay_UG.pdf
